specification.topic-statement: A collection of thin, build-mode-sensitive wrappers around POSIX system calls and C stdio operations that serve as link seams for test failure injection.

scope.in-scope: The wrapping mechanism itself: build-mode dispatch, weak symbol declaration, and pass-through delegation for all covered system calls.
scope.boundary: Tests replace any wrapped call by providing a strong symbol with the same name; this spec does not describe what those overrides do.
Callers receive raw return values and errno exactly as the underlying OS produces them; this layer adds no error transformation.
The wrappers contain commented-out diagnostic logging that does not execute; what that logging would have produced is out of scope.

field.name: create socket
field.type: (domain: int, type: int, protocol: int) -> int

field.name: bind address
field.type: (sockfd: int, addr: sockaddr*, addrlen: socklen_t) -> int

field.name: send data on socket
field.type: (sockfd: int, buf: const void*, len: size_t, flags: int) -> ssize_t

field.name: manipulate file descriptor
field.type: (fd: int, cmd: int, arg: int) -> int

field.name: write to descriptor
field.type: (fd: int, buf: const void*, count: size_t) -> ssize_t

field.name: read from descriptor
field.type: (fd: int, buf: void*, count: size_t) -> ssize_t

field.name: multiplex I/O
field.type: (nfds: int, readfds: fd_set*, writefds: fd_set*, exceptfds: fd_set*, timeout: timeval*) -> int

field.name: set terminal attributes
field.type: (fd: int, optional_actions: int, termios_p: const termios*) -> int

field.name: device control
field.type: (fd: int, request: unsigned long, argp: void*) -> int

field.name: set signal action
field.type: (signum: int, act: const sigaction*, oldact: sigaction*) -> int

field.name: wait for process state change
field.type: (pid: pid_t, status: int*, options: int) -> pid_t

field.name: open file as stream
field.type: (pathname: const char*, mode: const char*) -> FILE*

field.name: read from stream
field.type: (ptr: void*, size: size_t, nmemb: size_t, stream: FILE*) -> size_t

field.name: write to stream
field.type: (ptr: const void*, size: size_t, nmemb: size_t, stream: FILE*) -> size_t

field.name: seek in stream
field.type: (stream: FILE*, offset: long, whence: int) -> int

field.name: open process as stream
field.type: (command: const char*, mode: const char*) -> FILE*

field.name: rename file or directory
field.type: (oldpath: const char*, newpath: const char*) -> int

field.name: expand pathname pattern
field.type: (pattern: const char*, flags: int, errfunc: function pointer, pglob: glob_t*) -> int

behavior.build-mode-dispatch:
When the release build flag is set at compile time, every wrapper is compiled as a static inline function defined directly in the header. The body of the implementation file is excluded entirely from compilation. The result is zero-overhead inlining with no separate object code.
When the release build flag is absent (debug or test builds), every wrapper is compiled as a function with a weak linkage attribute. The implementation resides in a separate compilation unit. A test can supply a replacement strong symbol with the same name, which the linker will prefer over the weak symbol, thereby intercepting any call to the wrapped operation.
The function bodies are written identically in both the header (for release) and the implementation file (for debug). Keeping them synchronized is a manual responsibility; there is no single source of truth.
behavior.pass-through:
Every wrapper delegates directly to the underlying system call with identical arguments and returns the result unchanged. No error checking, logging, retry logic, or argument transformation is applied by the wrapper itself. The caller receives the same return value and errno state that the underlying OS would have produced.
The write and read wrappers contain commented-out diagnostic code that would have logged byte-level I/O activity to a debug channel, including a hex-escape formatter for non-printable bytes. This code is inert and produces no observable effect. The formatter itself is additionally marked as unused and only compiled when a secondary debug flag is present.
behavior.getenv-duplication:
The environment variable lookup wrapper is declared and defined in both this module and the standard library wrapper module. Both definitions are identical in behavior. In a given build, both object files are present; the weak symbol semantics mean the linker resolves to whichever definition it encounters first (or the test's strong override). There is no behavioral difference between the two copies.
Having the same weak symbol defined in two compilation units is normally a linker error for strong symbols. Weak symbols allow multiple definitions; the linker accepts all and selects one arbitrarily. In practice tests mock it by name and the duplication is invisible at runtime, but it represents an inconsistency in module ownership.
behavior.coverage-exclusion: The entire implementation file body is wrapped in a coverage-exclusion annotation. Because the release build inlines all functions from the header and the debug build relies on test mocking rather than line-by-line execution of the wrapper bodies themselves, the wrapper bodies are never the subject of direct coverage measurement. They are excluded from coverage reporting by convention.
behavior.unsafe-function: After all system headers are included, the implementation file includes a poison header that bans a set of unsafe C functions at compile time using a compiler pragma. Any use of the banned functions anywhere later in the same translation unit is a compile error. This enforcement is additive to the wrapper layer and does not affect the wrappers' own behavior.
